title: EY's private meeting with Syracuse city leaders raises questions on payroll modernization
summary: EY's October 2022 private 'wavespace' brainstorming session with senior Walsh administration officials at Firebarn Community Center raises transparency concerns about the $8.1M Payroll Modernization project. Whistleblower Advanced IT owner Susan Fahrenkrug alleged EY used her company as a 'pass-through' to secure the contract, with EY presenting timelines before her contract was signed. Mayor Walsh terminated EY's contract in January 2025, but questions persist about procurement practices and contract structure.

political_signal.description: Public investigative reporting reveals potential procurement irregularities and contract mismanagement in high-profile IT modernization project, with whistleblower allegations of vendor pass-through arrangements and exclusionary planning.

processing_notes: Article date is May 2025 (future date - verify); dollar amount incomplete ($8.1M extracted from context); key issue is contractor relationship opacity and potential circumvention of fair competition; CAO departure may indicate administrative response to controversy; article cites email documentation and named whistleblower providing substantive evidence
